{{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You '''do not''' need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You '''do not''' need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You '''do not''' need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You '''do not''' need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You '''do not''' need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You '''do not''' need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You '''do not''' need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You '''do not''' need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You '''do not''' need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You '''do not''' need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. Please note: if you are signed up on this list - expect to be assigned random new users as mentees. --------- * [[User:Sungodtemple]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! You can ask me questions about editing and other processes. I'll do my best to respond timely. * [[User:Sdkb]]|Hi, I'm Sdkb! I contribute to many different areas of Wikipedia where I see unmet needs, trying to improve the world's information ecosystem. Ask me anything about editing; I'm here to help! * [[User:Wugapodes]]|Welcome! I edit Wikipedia because I like sharing knowledge with others, so if you have questions feel free to ask me. I'm excited to work together! * [[User:Nosebagbear]]|Hi there! I improve articles and help users like you - let me know if you have any questions! * [[User:Trialpears]]|Hello! I edit in a lot of different areas where I see there are improvements to be made. I also enjoy helping new users navigate the confusing environment that is Wikipedia! * [[User:Ahmetlii]]|Welcome! I edit on different areas whenever there's a need of improvement, and try to help newcomers. Don't hesitate to ask questions about editing and other processes on Wikipedia, I will do my best to give a good answer. * [[User:Ipigott]]|I have experience of a wide variety of areas but am particularly keen on improving coverage of women. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. Please note: if you are signed up on this list - expect to be assigned random new users as mentees. --------- * [[User:Sungodtemple]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! You can ask me questions about editing and other processes. I'll do my best to respond timely. * [[User:Sdkb]]|Hi, I'm Sdkb! I contribute to many different areas of Wikipedia where I see unmet needs, trying to improve the world's information ecosystem. Ask me anything about editing; I'm here to help! * [[User:Wugapodes]]|Welcome! I edit Wikipedia because I like sharing knowledge with others, so if you have questions feel free to ask me. comment: Focus welcome message on editing Wikipedia matters

main.*:
{{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters. ([Check it [https://www.charactercountonline.com here].) Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. main.*:
{{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters. (Check it [https://www.charactercountonline.com here].) Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. Please note: if you are signed up on this list - expect to be assigned random new users as mentees. --------- * [[User:Sungodtemple]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! You can ask me questions about editing and other processes. I'll do my best to respond timely. * [[User:Sdkb]]|Hi, I'm Sdkb! I contribute to many different areas of Wikipedia where I see unmet needs, trying to improve the world's information ecosystem. Ask me anything about editing; I'm here to help! * [[User:Wugapodes]]|Welcome! I edit Wikipedia because I like sharing knowledge with others, so if you have questions feel free to ask me. I'm excited to work together! * [[User:Nosebagbear]]|Hi there! I improve articles and help users like you - let me know if you have any questions! * [[User:Trialpears]]|Hello! I edit in a lot of different areas where I see there are improvements to be made. I also enjoy helping new users navigate the confusing environment that is Wikipedia! * [[User:Ahmetlii]]|Welcome! I edit on different areas whenever there's a need of improvement, and try to help newcomers. Don't hesitate to ask questions about editing and other processes on Wikipedia, I will do my best to give a good answer. * [[User:Ipigott]]|I have experience of a wide variety of areas but am particularly keen on improving coverage of women. Please let me know how I can help you along. Happy editing! * [[User:Em-mustapha]]|Glad you are here and want to contribute to the sum of all human knowledge. How may I help you? * [[User:Bsoyka]]|Hey there! I contribute to many areas here on Wikipedia and I'm excited to help newcomers like you grow. Please let me know if you have any questions - I'm always happy to help and I look forward to meeting you! * [[User:Panini!]]|Hello! I'm Panini. If you have any questions about Wikipedia, feel free to ask. * [[User:Zoozaz1]]|Hi, I'm Zoozaz1! I'd be happy to answer any questions about Wikipedia you might have. I'll try to reply within a day. * [[User:TheSandDoctor]]|Hi, I'm TheSandDoctor! I was as confused as you are now. But asking questions to experienced users helped me. {{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. '''Who can become a mentor? '''
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a Mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|Extended Confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. (It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in Mainspace.) If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. '''How do I sign up?'''
Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters. (Check it [https://www.charactercountonline.com here].) Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will available as a mentor to many different types of new users. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) '''What happens next?'''
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees! - your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. Their post on your talk page will automatically begin "''Question from [username]''" and is tagged as a 'Mentorship module edit'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) '''Will I be swamped with questions?'''
This is unlikely to happen. Whilst you'll certainly see your Dashboard filling up with newly assigned usernames, by far the majority will make one edit or less. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. Hint: set your Dashboard filter to only display users who've made ''one edit or more'' in the last ''day'' or ''week'', and you will drastically reduce that list. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question&limit=100&days=14&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 Click Here]. '''Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?'''
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee 'How To Claim A Mentee']. '''Where can I leave feedback?'''
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]].
