__NOTOC__{{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. ;Who can become a mentor? If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|extended confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. ;How do I sign up? Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters. (Check it [https://www.charactercountonline.com here].) Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will appear as a mentor to new users with many different backgrounds. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, the topicon {{tlx|User mentor topicon}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) ;What happens next? Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees – your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. ;How will I know if a question comes from one of my mentees?''' When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their dashboard, their question will appear on ''your'' talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading: :"''Question from [username]''" That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) ;Will I be swamped with questions? This is unlikely to happen. By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further. Your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] lets you indicate if you are available or 'Away', and to select how many mentees are assigned to you. Bear in mind: the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here]. ;Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to? In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]]. ;Where can I leave feedback? You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. ;How can I leave mentorship? If you remove your name from the list, you will not get assigned new mentees, but you will keep your current mentees.
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please remove your name from the list below, and then visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. Please note: if you are signed up on this list, expect to be assigned random new users as mentees. --------- * [[User:Sungodtemple]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! You can ask me questions about editing and other processes. I'll do my best to respond timely. * [[User:Sdkb]]|Hi, I'm Sdkb! I contribute to many different areas of Wikipedia where I see unmet needs, trying to improve the world's information ecosystem. Ask me anything about editing; I'm here to help! * [[User:Wugapodes]]|Welcome! I edit Wikipedia because I like sharing knowledge with others, so if you have questions feel free to ask me. I'm excited to work together! * [[User:Nosebagbear]]|Hi there! I improve articles and help users like you - let me know if you have any questions! * [[User:Trialpears]]|Hello! I edit in a lot of different areas where I see there are improvements to be made. I also enjoy helping new users navigate the confusing environment that is Wikipedia! * [[User:Ahmetlii]]|Welcome! I edit on different areas whenever there's a need of improvement, and try to help newcomers. Don't hesitate to ask questions about editing and other processes on Wikipedia, I will do my best to give a good answer. * [[User:Ipigott]]|I have experience of a wide variety of areas but am particularly keen on improving coverage of women. Please let me know how I can help you along. Happy editing! * [[User:Em-mustapha]]|Glad you are here and want to contribute to the sum of all human knowledge. How may I help you? * [[User:Bsoyka]]|Hey there! I contribute to many areas here on Wikipedia and I'm excited to help newcomers like you grow. Please let me know if you have any questions - I'm always happy to help and I look forward to meeting you! * [[User:Panini!]]|Hello! I'm Panini. Although I'm active in contributing to video game articles, I've learned the ropes and am here to answer your questions. If you have any, please ask! * [[User:Ampimd]]|Hello, I edit a wide variety of articles and topics, I hope I can lend a helping hand to you. Enjoy! * [[User:Tol]]|Welcome! I'm another Wikipedia editor, and am looking forward to get to know you. Ask me if you have any questions! * [[User:Calliopejen1]]|Hi! I'm a longtime Wikipedia editor and love teaching others how to contribute. Let me know if you have any questions! * [[User:Berrely]]|Hi, I'm Berrely and I've been here for a while. I like editing topics related to England and video games, and I often make diagrams and graphics for articles. Feel free to ask any questions! * [[User:Bilorv]]|Hi! I'm happy to answer any questions you have, and none are too silly to ask. I was a newcomer once and I know how much new information there is at first, but I can help you find a place to start. * [[User:Elli]]|Hi! I'm a somewhat experienced editor happy to work with people interested in editing here. * [[User:Isabelle Belato]]|Hi and welcome to Wikipedia! My name is Isabelle and I'm glad to see you are interested in helping with the project. If you have any questions, feel free to ask! * [[User:DanCherek]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! I like editing articles about literature and music, but feel free to ask me about anything — I'd love to help you get started. * [[User:ARoseWolf]]|Hello and welcome! My name is Asareel and I am here to assist you with navigating and editing Wikipedia. Please do not hesitate to ask me any questions you may have. * [[User:GenQuest]]|I can copyedit your new article(s) and creation(s) in such a way that you learn to avoid the pitfalls many new users face while beginning to edit here. * [[User:Michael D. Turnbull]]|I mainly contribute to science-related topics but I'll try to help you with other questions, including use of Wikimedia Commons for uploading images. * [[User:Pyrrho the Skipper]]| Hi and welcome to Wikipedia! I have a wide variety of interests, and want to help editors have confidence and knowledge in their journey. Ask me literally anything, anytime! * [[User:Qwerfjkl]]|Hi, I'm Qwerfjkl! If you have any questions about Wikipedia, feel free to ask. * [[User:CaptainEek]]|Howdy hello! I'm CaptainEek and I sail the high seas of Wikipedia looking for users to help! Feel free to leave a message in a bottle for me, and welcome aboard matey. * [[User:lajmmoore]]| Hi there, lovely to meet you, and looking forward to supporting your edits! * [[User:Ezlev]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! I love helping to improve this free source of information, and I'm so glad you want to help too. Ask me anything and I'll do my best to help! * [[User:Pahunkat]]|Hello and welcome! I'm Pahunkat, an editor here on Wikipedia. Please feel free to ask me any questions you have, and I'll try my best to help! * [[User:EpicPupper]]|Hi, I'm EpicPupper! If you have any questions about Wikipedia, feel free to ask. * [[User:Zoozaz1]]|Hi, I'm Zoozaz1! I'd be happy to answer any questions about Wikipedia you might have. I'll try to reply within a day. * [[User:TheSandDoctor]]|Hi, I'm TheSandDoctor! I was as confused as you are now. But asking questions to experienced users helped me. Please feel free to ask me any question! * [[User:Pbsouthwood]]|I will help if I can, otherwise will try to point you to someone else who is more expert in whatever the problem may be. I am in UTC+2 time zone. My interests are mainly in science, technology and underwater diving. * [[User:Justiyaya]]|Hi, I'm Justiyaya! If you have any questions about Wikipedia, feel free to ask. * [[User:Chlod]]|Hiya! I'm Chlod, and I hope you enjoy your stay as a Wikipedia editor! Let me know if you have any questions or concerns, and I'll come running! * [[User:JavaHurricane]]|Hi there! Welcome to Wikipedia. I've been editing for a few years now, and will do my best to help you. * [[User:Rotideypoc41352]]|Hello and welcome! Please feel free to drop by my talk page, introduce yourself, and let me know what you led you to click that "Edit" button. * [[User:Extraordinary Writ]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! Do feel free to ask me any questions you might have about editing: I'd be glad to help. * [[User:LEvalyn]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! I've been editing for a few years and having a lot of fun, I am happy to answer questions or help however I can! * [[User:I dream of horses]]|Hello! I'm I dream of horses, and have been editing for over a decade! Ask me anything about Wikipedia. * [[User:Blaze Wolf]]|Hello! I'm here to help new users in understanding and editing Wikipedia constructively. * [[User:ZI Jony]]|Hello, Welcome to Wikipedia! I've been editing for a few years. Do feel free to ask me any questions you might have about editing, I am happy to answer your questions or help you however I can! * [[User:FormalDude]]|I'm happy to help answer your questions! I edit Wikipedia daily and am quick at responding. * [[User:Nick Moyes]]|Hello! If you need any help or guidance as you get started here, just click the button below and ask me a question. So long as it's related to editing Wikipedia, I'm happy to help you. Cheers, Nick. * [[User:Aerin17]]|Hello and welcome to Wikipedia! Feel free to ask me any questions you may have-- I'm always happy to help. * [[User:Gerald Waldo Luis]]|Hey, welcome! :) If you're a newcomer and you'd like to ask questions about editing, I'd be happy to help! Keep in mind my timezone is UTC+7, though I sometimes stay up. * [[User:David notMD]]|My advice likely most useful to people who want to contribute to articles in the medical/health/nutrition/alternative medicine arenas. * [[User:Zippybonzo]]|I am happy to help you with anything at all. If you need any help, I am the person to ask. * [[User:Lightbluerain]]|Hello and Welcome to Wikipedia! Please feel free to ask me any questions you have about editing Wikipedia. I'd be very happy to help you out. * [[User:Liance]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! I'm Liance, a fellow editor who has been here for a while. If you have any questions I am happy to help! * [[User:Martin Urbanec]]|Hello, I am Martin and I love to help new wikipedians. If you have any questions, please reach out to me. Happy editing! * [[User:Dreamyshade]]|Hi, I'm Britta. I enjoy helping people learn how to edit Wikipedia, and I'd be happy to answer your questions! * [[User:Alyo]]|Hi! I'm Alyo, and I promise no question is too simple to ask. I'm happy to help with anything you may need, so just let me know! * [[User:DoubleGrazing]]|Hey there, and welcome to Wikipedia! If you need any help or have questions, don't hesitate to ask, and I'll do my best to assist. * [[User:Rubbish computer]]|Hi and welcome to Wikipedia. Ask me anything and I'll do my best to answer. * [[User:Lectonar]]|Welcome, and feel free to ask me anything about editing Wikipedia. I will do my best to help YOU. * [[User:GoingBatty]]|Hi there, and welcome to Wikipedia! How can I help you today? * [[User:TheAafi]]|Hi there, and welcome to the Wikipedia! Ask me anything and I'll try my best to help. I'm just a question ahead. * [[User:EDG 543]]|Hullo there, friend! I'm EDG 543, your mentor. If you ever have any questions or concerns, any at all, I'm here for you! * [[User:Firefangledfeathers]]|Thanks for editing Wikipedia! Happy to help out however I can. * [[User:Ferien]]|Hello, I'm Ferien! Please feel free to ask me any question and I will get back to you as soon as possible. :) * [[User:Kaleeb18]]|Hello there, I’m here to help out with any problem or question you might have about Wikipedia. * [[User:Cullen328]]|I would be happy to answer any questions you have about editing Wikipedia. * [[User:Gråbergs Gråa Sång]]|Hello! If you have a question about editing Wikipedia, I'll do my best to answer. I'm not on WP 24/7, but check in most days. * [[User:Cassiopeia]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! I'm Cassiopeia. I am here to help if you have any questions about editing Wikipedia. * [[User:Oshwah]]|Hi, I'm Oshwah. I'm here to help you! No, seriously, I really am! I've been an editor (just like you!) on Wikipedia for over 15 years. Need help with something? Please, don't be a stranger! I'll be more than happy to lend a hand and assist you with anything that you need. Wikipedia is a great place to volunteer and be a part of. Learning how everything works around here can be challenging for new users; don't worry, I have your back! Now, let's do something awesome, and let's make Wikipedia a fun community to be a part of. :-) * [[User:CaroleHenson]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! I am CaroleHenson. I am happy to answer questions about Wikipedia. *[[:User:Marchjuly]]|Hello and welcome to Wikipedia. Feel free to ask me for assistance if there's something about Wikipedia that you're having trouble understanding. *[[User:Pichemist]]|Welcome to Wikipedia. Don't hesitate to reach out to me if you have any questions and I promise I won't take too long to reply. * [[User:Capsulecap]]|Hello! Capsulecap's my name and Wikipedia's my game. If you ever need help with editing or using Wikipedia, just ask me a question. * [[User:Hajoon0102]]|Hello and welcome to Wikipedia! Question to me when you have a question. * [[User:SkyWolf369]]|Heya, I am SkyWolf I primarily do Anti-Vandalism and am more than willing to help teach new users! * [[User:IAmChaos]]|Hello, I edit in a few areas across Wikipedia, and would love to help you out. Just hit the button to ask me a question * [[User:Melecie]]|Hi and welcome! Need a hand on your edits or understanding the guidelines? Drop me a question and I'll do my best to help ya! Happy editing <3 * [[User:Kpddg]]|Welcome! I'm glad to help! *[[User:The4lines]]|Hello, and welcome! I’ll be happy to help with any questions you might have! * [[User:Perfect4th]]|Hello there, and welcome to Wikipedia! I'm happy to help out with any editing questions you might have! * [[User:Zindor]]|Hi, my name is Zindor. If you have any questions about editing Wikipedia then feel free to ask me, i'm here to help. * [[User:Goldsztajn]]| Greetings, happy to assist with questions related to editing on Wikipedia. * [[User:Clovermoss]]|Hi, I'm Clovermoss. Welcome to Wikipedia! If you have any questions, because things can get confusing here, feel free to ask me. I'll do my best to try and help. * [[User:Shellwood]]|Hello and welcome! I'm Shellwood, feel free to ask me anything about editing Wikipedia and I'll do my best to help you. * [[User:Idoghor Melody]]|Hi, I'm Comr Melody Idoghor and I am here to help you in anyway I can. * [[User:SHB2000]]|Hello and welcome to Wikipedia! Feel free to ask me any questions that you may have. * [[User:Dr_vulpes]]|Welcome to Wikipedia, I'm a real person here to help you if you have any questions or need some help. Please reach out if you need anything, happy editing! * [[User:Alpha3031]]|I am here to help you navigate Wikipedia. If you have any questions at all, please feel free to ask! * [[User:MPGuy2824]]|Hello and welcome to Wikipedia! Feel free to ask me any questions that you may have. * [[User:The Night Watch]]|New around here? Welcome. I'm the Night Watch, willing to offer advice to editors such as yourself. If you have any questions, go ahead and ask me. * [[User:ThadeusOfNazereth]]|Hi, I'm Thadeus! I was a new user once and remember how daunting Wikipedia was, so feel free to ask my any questions you have :)

__NOTOC__{{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. ;Who can become a mentor? If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|extended confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. ;How do I sign up? Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters. (Check it [https://www.charactercountonline.com here].) Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will appear as a mentor to new users with many different backgrounds. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, the topicon {{tlx|User mentor topicon}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) ;What happens next? Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees – your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. ;How will I know if a question comes from one of my mentees?''' When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their dashboard, their question will appear on ''your'' talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading: :"''Question from [username]''" That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) ;Will I be swamped with questions? This is unlikely to happen. By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further. Your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] lets you indicate if you are available or 'Away', and to select how many mentees are assigned to you. Bear in mind: the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here]. ;Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to? In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]]. ;Where can I leave feedback? You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. ;How can I leave mentorship? If you remove your name from the list, you will not get assigned new mentees, but you will keep your current mentees.
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please remove your name from the list below, and then visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. Please note: if you are signed up on this list, expect to be assigned random new users as mentees. --------- * [[User:Sungodtemple]]|Welcome to Wikipedia! You can ask me questions about editing and other processes. I'll do my best to respond timely. * [[User:Sdkb]]|Hi, I'm Sdkb! __NOTOC__{{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. ;Who can become a mentor? If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|extended confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. ;How do I sign up? Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters. (Check it [https://www.charactercountonline.com here].) Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will appear as a mentor to new users with many different backgrounds. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, the topicon {{tlx|User mentor topicon}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) ;What happens next? Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees – your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. ;How will I know if a question comes from one of my mentees?''' When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their dashboard, their question will appear on ''your'' talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading: :"''Question from [username]''" That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) ;Will I be swamped with questions? This is unlikely to happen. By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further. Your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] lets you indicate if you are available or 'Away', and to select how many mentees are assigned to you. Bear in mind: the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here]. ;Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to? In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]]. ;Where can I leave feedback? You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. ;How can I leave mentorship? If you remove your name from the list, you will not get assigned new mentees, but you will keep your current mentees.
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please remove your name from the list below, and then visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. I'm Winner 42, please reach out to me with any questions you have!

__NOTOC__{{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page. ;Who can become a mentor? If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a mentor. Thank you! You need to be [[WP:XC|extended confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself. ;How do I sign up? Mentors should sign up below with a short welcome message using the following formatting: [[User:Name]]|Description. Please use only this syntax; DO NOT insert any links or wikitext in your welcome message or it may affect how all the messages appear. Please limit it to 240 characters. (Check it [https://www.charactercountonline.com here].) Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will appear as a mentor to new users with many different backgrounds. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. (If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, the topicon {{tlx|User mentor topicon}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.) ;What happens next? Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees – your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. ;How will I know if a question comes from one of my mentees?''' When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their dashboard, their question will appear on ''your'' talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading: :"''Question from [username]''" That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ]) ;Will I be swamped with questions? This is unlikely to happen. By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further. Your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] lets you indicate if you are available or 'Away', and to select how many mentees are assigned to you. Bear in mind: the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here]. ;Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to? In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]]. ;Where can I leave feedback? You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. ;How can I leave mentorship? If you remove your name from the list, you will not get assigned new mentees, but you will keep your current mentees.
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please remove your name from the list below, and then visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. Please note: if you are signed up on this list, expect to be assigned random new users as mentees. --------- To add or remove your name from this list, please visit [[Special:MentorDashboard]]. {{Special:ManageMentors}}

<noinclude>__NOTOC__{{pp-30-500|small=yes}}{{pp-move-indef}}</noinclude>
This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please add your name and a brief welcome message at the bottom of the page.

;Who can become a mentor?
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a mentor. Thank you!

You need to be [[WP:XC|extended confirmed]] to edit this list at the present time. It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before adding yourself.

;How do I sign up?
Visit [[Special:EnrollAsMentor]] to sign up. You will need to specify a short welcome message. Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will appear as a mentor to new users with many different backgrounds. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. 

(If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, the topicon {{tlx|User mentor topicon}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.)

;What happens next?
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. 

You <u>do not</u> need to proactively welcome your mentees – your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. 

;How will I know if a question comes from one of my mentees?'''
When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their dashboard, their question will appear on ''your'' talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading:
:"''Question from [username]''"  
That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ])

;Will I be swamped with questions?
This is unlikely to happen. By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further. Your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] lets you indicate if you are available or 'Away', and to select how many mentees are assigned to you.
Bear in mind: the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. 

For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here]. 

;Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), add your name instead to [[WP:Growth Team features/Mentor list/Manual|this alternative list]] (but NOT to both!). Then follow [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]].

;Where can I leave feedback?
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]].

;How can I leave mentorship?
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. 


Administrators may manage the list of mentors and their settings at [[Special:ManageMentors]]

{{Special:ManageMentors}}

;Who can become a mentor?

You need have at least 500 edits and have had an account for 90 days to sign up as a mentor. It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before signing up.

Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. 

(If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, the topicon {{tlx|User mentor topicon}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.)

;What happens next?
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']]. You can reach it via a new link at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own 'Homepage' tab. 

You <u>do not</u> need to proactively welcome your mentees – your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. 

;How will I know if a question comes from one of my mentees?'''
When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their dashboard, their question will appear on ''your'' talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading:
:"''Question from [username]''"  
That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ])

;Will I be swamped with questions?
This is unlikely to happen. By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further. Your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] lets you indicate if you are available or 'Away', and to select how many mentees are assigned to you.
Bear in mind: the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. 

For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here]. 

;Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), you can sign up as a mentor following the above process, then visit [[Special:MentorDashboard]] and set the "Number of mentees assigned to me" dropdown to "None". Then follow [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]].

;Where can I leave feedback?
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]].

;How can I leave mentorship?
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. 


Administrators may manage the list of mentors and their settings at [[Special:ManageMentors]]

{{Special:ManageMentors}}

When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their dashboard, their question will appear on ''your'' talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading:
:"''Question from [username]''"  
That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki FAQ])

;Will I be swamped with questions?
This is unlikely to happen. By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further. Your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] lets you indicate if you are available or 'Away', and to select how many mentees are assigned to you.
Bear in mind: the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. 

For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here]. 

;Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?
In general, no. However, if you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), you can sign up as a mentor following the above process, then visit [[Special:MentorDashboard]] and set the "Number of mentees assigned to me" dropdown to "None". Then follow [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]].

;Where can I leave feedback?
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]].

;How can I leave mentorship?
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. 


Administrators may manage the list of mentors and their settings at [[Special:ManageMentors]]

<noinclude>__NOTOC__</noinclude>
This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please read the few Q&A below, and visit [[Special:EnrollAsMentor]] to add your name to the list.

;Why Mentorship?
When you arrive to a new place, it is always better to have a guide. Wikipedia is a complicated ecosystem, with its own set of technical subtleties, cultural habits and social interactions. Getting a mentor you can contact directly when you arrive helps you to make better first steps.

;Who can become a mentor?
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a mentor. Thank you!

You need have at least 500 edits and have had an account for 90 days to sign up as a mentor. It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before signing up.

;How do I sign up?
Visit [[Special:EnrollAsMentor]] to sign up. You will need to specify a short welcome message. Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will appear as a mentor to new users with many different backgrounds. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. 

(If you wish, you may add the Userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, the topicon {{tlx|User mentor topicon}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.)

;What happens next?
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']] after a few hours. You can reach it via a new link in your personal user menu at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own [[Special:Homepage|'Homepage']] tab. 

You <u>do not</u> need to proactively welcome your mentees – your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. 

;How will I know if a question comes from one of my mentees?'''
When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their 'Homepage', their question will appear on ''your'' talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading:
:"''Question from [username]''"  
That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [[mw:Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki|FAQ]])

;Will I be swamped with questions?
This is unlikely to happen. A Mentor is given to each new account, but about 1 user over 500 new accounts contact their mentor (based on the average number of questions asked by newcomers at wikis where Mentorship is deployed). However, it is not yet the case at English Wikipedia, where only 10% of new accounts get a mentor ([[Phab:T323048|as of Feb 2023]]), due to a lack of mentors.

Bear in mind: the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. 

For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here]. 

;Can I set myself as Away from mentorship for a few days?
By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further. Your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] lets you indicate if you are available or 'Away', and to select how many mentees are assigned to you.

;Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?
In general, no. However, you can claim a mentee using [[Special:ClaimMentee]] (see [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How_to_claim_a_mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]]). This tool should be use if you find a mention who work on similar topics as yours, or if you have an ongoing conversation with them.

If you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), you can sign up as a mentor following the above process, then visit [[Special:MentorDashboard]] and set the "Number of mentees assigned to me" dropdown to "None". You can still use [[Special:ClaimMentee]] but no new accounts will get your username.

;Where can I leave feedback?
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]].

;How can I leave mentorship?
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. 

Administrators may manage the list of mentors and their settings at [[Special:ManageMentors]]

{{Special:ManageMentors}}

; Who can become a mentor?
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a mentor. Thank you!

You need have at least 500 edits and have had an account for 90 days to sign up as a mentor. It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before signing up.

; How do I sign up?
Visit [[Special:EnrollAsMentor]] to sign up. You will need to specify a short welcome message. Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will appear as a mentor to new users with many different backgrounds. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. 

(If you wish, you may add the userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, the topicon {{tlx|User mentor topicon}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.)

; What happens next?
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']] after a few hours. You can reach it via a new link in your personal user menu at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own [[Special:Homepage|'Homepage']] tab. 

You <u>do not</u> need to proactively welcome your mentees – your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. 

; How will I know if a question comes from one of my mentees?'''
When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their 'Homepage', their question will appear on ''your'' talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading:
:"''Question from [username]''"  
That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [[mw:Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki|FAQ]])

; Will I be swamped with questions?
This is unlikely to happen. A Mentor is given to each new account, but about 1 user over 500 new accounts contact their mentor (based on the average number of questions asked by newcomers at wikis where Mentorship is deployed). However, it is not yet the case at English Wikipedia, where only 10% of new accounts get a mentor ([[Phab:T323048|as of Feb 2023]]), due to a lack of mentors.

Bear in mind: the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. 

For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here]. 

; Can I set myself as Away from mentorship for a few days?

Yes: please visit your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] and change your "Mentor status" in the "Settings" module to 'Away'. You will be asked to fill the length of your time off. You will be resumed as 'Active' automatically when the period of time you defined is over. You can return as active, or either shorten or extend this time off anytime by visiting your dashboard.

No new mentee will be assigned to you during your time off. Your mentees will be informed of your absence when they want to contact you, and they will be redirected to a different mentor. However, some newcomers who already started a conversation with you, or the ones that contact you by directly visiting your user talk page, may still leave you a message.

If you plan to be off for more than a few weeks, please consider to leave mentorship instead (and return!).

; How newcomers are filtered at my mentor dashboard?
By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further.

; Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?
In general, no. However, you can claim a mentee using [[Special:ClaimMentee]] (see [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How to claim a mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]]). This tool can be used if you find a mentee who works on similar topics to you, or if you have an ongoing conversation with them.

If you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), you can sign up as a mentor following the above process, then visit [[Special:MentorDashboard]] and set the "Number of mentees assigned to me" dropdown to "None". You can still use [[Special:ClaimMentee]] but no new accounts will get your username.


; How can I leave mentorship?
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. 

Administrators may manage the list of mentors and their settings at [[Special:ManageMentors]]

;Where can I leave feedback?
;Where can I know more about mentorship and Growth features?
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. You can also visit [[mw:Growth/FAQ|Growth's FAQ]].

{{Special:ManageMentors}}

; How can I leave mentorship?
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. 

Administrators may manage the list of mentors and their settings at [[Special:ManageMentors]]

;Where can I leave feedback?
;Where can I know more about mentorship and Growth features?
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. You can also visit [[mw:Growth/FAQ|Growth's FAQ]].

{{Special:ManageMentors}}

<noinclude>__NOTOC__</noinclude>
This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please read the Q&A below, and visit '''[[Special:EnrollAsMentor]]''' to add your name to the list.

; Why mentorship?
When you arrive to a new place, it is always better to have a guide. Wikipedia is a complicated ecosystem, with its own set of technical subtleties, cultural habits and social interactions. Getting a mentor you can contact directly when you arrive helps you to make better first steps.

; Who can become a mentor?
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a mentor. Thank you!

You need have at least 500 edits and have had an account for 90 days to sign up as a mentor. It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before signing up.

; How do I sign up?
Visit [[Special:EnrollAsMentor]] to sign up. You will need to specify a short welcome message. Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will appear as a mentor to new users with many different backgrounds. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. 

(If you wish, you may add the userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, the topicon {{tlx|User mentor topicon}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.)

; What happens next?
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']] after a few hours. You can reach it via a new link in your personal user menu at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own [[Special:Homepage|'Homepage']] tab. 

You <u>do not</u> need to proactively welcome your mentees – your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. 

; How will I know if a question comes from one of my mentees?'''
When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their 'Homepage', their question will appear on ''your'' talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading:
:"''Question from [username]''"  
That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [[mw:Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki|FAQ]])

; Will I be swamped with questions?
This is unlikely to happen. A Mentor is given to each new account, but about 1 user over 500 new accounts contact their mentor (based on the average number of questions asked by newcomers at wikis where Mentorship is deployed). However, it is not yet the case at English Wikipedia, where only 25% of new accounts get a mentor ([[Phab:T341399|as of July 2023]]), due to a lack of mentors.

Bear in mind: the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. 

*For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here]. 
*For a list of curently unanswered qustions over the last 30 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidepreviousrevisions=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=250&days=30&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here].

; Can I set myself as Away from mentorship for a few days?

Yes: please visit your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] and change your "Mentor status" in the "Settings" module to 'Away'. You will be asked to fill the length of your time off. You will be resumed as 'Active' automatically when the period of time you defined is over. You can return as active, or either shorten or extend this time off anytime by visiting your dashboard.

No new mentee will be assigned to you during your time off. Your mentees will be informed of your absence when they want to contact you, and they will be redirected to a different mentor. However, some newcomers who already started a conversation with you, or the ones that contact you by directly visiting your user talk page, may still leave you a message.

If you plan to be off for more than a few weeks, please consider to leave mentorship instead (and return!).

; How are newcomers filtered at my mentor dashboard?
By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further.

; Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?
In general, no. However, you can claim a mentee using [[Special:ClaimMentee]] (see [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How to claim a mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]]). This tool can be used if you find a mentee who works on similar topics to you, or if you have an ongoing conversation with them.

If you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), you can sign up as a mentor following the above process, then visit [[Special:MentorDashboard]] and set the "Number of mentees assigned to me" dropdown to "None". You can still use [[Special:ClaimMentee]] but no new accounts will get your username.


; How can I leave mentorship?
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. 

Administrators may manage the list of mentors and their settings at [[Special:ManageMentors]]

;Where can I leave feedback?
;Where can I know more about mentorship and Growth features?
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. You can also visit [[mw:Growth/FAQ|Growth's FAQ]].

{{Special:ManageMentors}}

You need to have at least 500 edits and have had an account for 90 days to sign up as a mentor. It is also expected that a significant proportion of your 500 minimum edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before signing up.

; How do I sign up?
Visit [[Special:EnrollAsMentor]] to sign up. You will need to specify a short welcome message. Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will appear as a mentor to new users with many different backgrounds. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]]. 

(If you wish, you may add the userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, the topicon {{tlx|User mentor topicon}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.)

; What happens next?
Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']] after a few hours. You can reach it via a new link in your personal user menu at the top of every page. Each new user (mentee) assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own [[Special:Homepage|'Homepage']] tab. 

You <u>do not</u> need to proactively welcome your mentees – your invitation message simply invites ''them'' to ask ''you'' questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. However, if you wish to welcome your mentees anyway, you can use the {{tls|Mentor welcome}} or {{tls|Mentor welcome-autosign}} templates.

; How will I know if a question comes from one of my mentees?'''
When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their 'Homepage', their question will appear on ''your'' talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading:
:"''Question from [username]''"  
That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [[mw:Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki|FAQ]])

; Will I be swamped with questions?
This is unlikely to happen. A Mentor is given to each new account, but about 1 user over 500 new accounts contact their mentor (based on the average number of questions asked by newcomers at wikis where Mentorship is deployed). However, it is not yet the case at English Wikipedia, where only 50% of new accounts get a mentor ([[Phab:T341399|as of October 2023]]), due to a lack of mentors.

Bear in mind: the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. They might ask for things that are written on an help page or a policy page: don't forget that the vast majority of the world has no clue of how Wikipedia works. 

*For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here]. 
*For a list of currently unanswered questions over the last 30 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidepreviousrevisions=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=250&days=30&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here].

; I respond to questions, and then the mentee doesn't return. It is quite unpleasant.
Most newcomers aren't like mentors: they don't visit Wikipedia everyday. Some will return the day after, some other the week after (Wikipedia can be a recreational activity for the weekend), some other months after. Consider this when you curate your talk page: some mentees won't contact you again as they can't find your archived response.

{{anchor|away}}
; Can I set myself as Away from mentorship for a few days?

Yes: please visit your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] and change your "Mentor status" in the "Settings" module to 'Away'. You will be asked to fill the length of your time off. You will be resumed as 'Active' automatically when the period of time you defined is over. You can return as active, or either shorten or extend this time off anytime by visiting your dashboard.

No new mentee will be assigned to you during your time off. Your mentees will be informed of your absence when they want to contact you, and they will be redirected to a different mentor. However, some newcomers who already started a conversation with you, or the ones that contact you by directly visiting your user talk page, may still leave you a message.

If you plan to be off for more than a few weeks, please consider to leave mentorship instead (and return!).

; How are newcomers filtered at my mentor dashboard?
By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further.

; Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to?
In general, no. However, you can claim a mentee using [[Special:ClaimMentee]] (see [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How to claim a mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]]). This tool can be used if you find a mentee who works on similar topics to you, or if you have an ongoing conversation with them.

If you only want to act as mentor to a few ''specific'' users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), you can sign up as a mentor following the above process, then visit [[Special:MentorDashboard]] and set the "Number of mentees assigned to me" dropdown to "None". You can still use [[Special:ClaimMentee]] but no new accounts will get your username.

; How can I leave mentorship?
If you want to leave mentorship for good, and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. If you plan to return after a short absence, consider [[#away|pausing mentorship instead]]

Administrators may manage the list of mentors and their settings at [[Special:ManageMentors]]

;Where can I leave feedback?
;Where can I know more about mentorship and Growth features?
You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. You can also visit [[mw:Growth/FAQ|Growth's FAQ]].

{{Special:ManageMentors}}

<noinclude>__NOTOC__</noinclude>
This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please read the Q&A below, and visit '''[[Special:EnrollAsMentor]]''' to add your name to the list.

; Why mentorship?
When you arrive to a new place, it is always better to have a guide. Wikipedia is a complicated ecosystem, with its own set of technical subtleties, cultural habits and social interactions. Getting a mentor you can contact directly when you arrive helps you to make better first steps.

; Who can become a mentor?
If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a mentor. Thank you!

You need to have at least 500 edits and have had an account for 90 days to sign up as a mentor. It is also expected that a significant proportion of those 500 edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before signing up.

:"''Question from [username]''"

An haifi Abdulhamid abubakar kurya a garin Kurya Madaro, cikin iyalin (ldris Abdullahi kurya) Abubakar Idris Kurya, da Hauwa’u Suleiman. Sun yi mishi tarbiyya da nuna Masa ƙauna, sun kuma koya mishi darajar gaskiya, haƙuri, da kuma taimakon juna.
Ya  girma a cikin al’adun Hausawa, inda aka koya mana mu girmama manya, mu yi aiki tukuru, kuma mu riƙe addininmu. Waɗannan dabi’u sun zama ginshiƙi na rayuwatai.
Yace na gode wa Allah da ya ba ni damar samun irin wannan tarbiyya da kuma dama ta samun ilimi. Ina kuma gode wa iyayena da dangina da suka yi min goyon baya. Zan ci gaba da kokari na domin na yi wa al’ummata da ƙasata hidima.”

__NOTOC__ {{shortcut|WP:GT/ML}} This is the list of mentors for the [[Wikipedia:Growth Team features|Growth Team features]]. If you would like to be available to help new users, please read the Q&A below, and visit '''[[Special:EnrollAsMentor]]''' to add your name to the list. ; Why mentorship? :When you arrive to a new place, it is always better to have a guide. Wikipedia is a complicated ecosystem, with its own set of technical subtleties, cultural habits and social interactions. Getting a mentor you can contact directly when you arrive helps you to make better first steps. ; Who can become a mentor? :If you are sufficiently experienced to answer basic editing questions from new users, and enjoy helping people in a friendly and supportive manner, then you are an ideal person to be a mentor. Thank you!

You need to have at least 500 edits and have had an account for 90 days to sign up as a mentor. It is also expected that a significant proportion of those 500 edits will have been made in mainspace. If you don't yet meet this key requirement, please gain a bit more experience before signing up.

; How do I sign up? :Visit [[Special:EnrollAsMentor]] to sign up. You will need to specify a short welcome message. Avoid mentioning your own subject interests in your welcome message, as you will appear as a mentor to new users with many different backgrounds. Full instructions are at [[mw:Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor|Growth/Communities/How to introduce yourself as a mentor]].

(If you wish, you may add the userbox {{tlx|User mentor}}, the topicon {{tlx|User mentor topicon}}, or '[[:Category:Wikipedians who mentor new users]]' to your own user page.)

; What happens next? :Once you have signed up here, you will start to be randomly assigned users as 'mentees'. Their names will begin to appear in your new [[Special:MentorDashboard|'Mentor dashboard']] after a few hours. You can reach it via a new link in your personal user menu at the top of every page. Each mentee assigned to you will see ''your'' welcome message in a 'Your mentor' box on ''their'' own [[Special:Homepage|'Homepage']] tab.

You do not need to proactively welcome your mentees – your invitation message simply invites them to ask you questions should they ever encounter difficulties and need help. However, if you wish to welcome your mentees anyway, you can use the {{tls|Mentor welcome}} or {{tls|Mentor welcome-autosign}} templates.

; How will I know if a question comes from one of my mentees? : When someone clicks the ''"Ask your mentor a question"'' button on their 'Homepage', their question will appear on your talk page. It will always appear with the following subject heading:
"''Question from [username]''"
That username will always be hyperlinked, and the edit will be tagged in your page history as a 'Mentorship module edit' or 'Mentorship panel question'. (For further information see this [[mw:Growth/FAQ#mentoring Mediawiki|FAQ]]) ; Will I be swamped with questions? : This is unlikely to happen. A Mentor is given to each new account, but about 1 user over 500 new accounts contact their mentor (based on the average number of questions asked by newcomers at wikis where Mentorship is deployed). However, it is not yet the case at English Wikipedia, where only 50% of new accounts get a mentor ([[Phab:T341399|as of October 2023]]), due to a lack of mentors.

Bear in mind, the majority of new users will make just one edit. Of the remainder, most will be OK as they are. Of those needing help, some will find their way to the [[WP:TH|Teahouse]] or get solutions elsewhere. Only a small number will ever seek your help as a mentor, and we hope you will be there for them when they do. They might ask for things that are written on an help page or a policy page: don't forget that the vast majority of the world has no clue of how Wikipedia works.

  • For a list of every question asked of all mentors over the last 14 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=100&days=14&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here].
  • For a list of currently unanswered questions over the last 30 days, [https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?hidebots=1&hidepreviousrevisions=1&hidecategorization=1&hideWikibase=1&tagfilter=mentorship+module+question%7Cmentorship+panel+question&limit=250&days=30&enhanced=1&title=Special:RecentChanges&urlversion=2 click here].
; I respond to questions, and then the mentee doesn't return. It is quite unpleasant. :Most newcomers aren't like mentors: they don't visit Wikipedia everyday. Some will return the day after, some other the week after (Wikipedia can be a recreational activity for the weekend), some other months after. Consider this when you curate your talk page: some mentees won't contact you again as they can't find your archived response. ; {{anchor|away}} Can I set myself as Away from mentorship for a few days? :Yes: please visit your [[Special:MentorDashboard|Mentor Dashboard]] and change your "Mentor status" in the "Settings" module to 'Away'. You will be asked to fill the length of your time off. You will be resumed as 'Active' automatically when the period of time you defined is over. You can return as active, or either shorten or extend this time off anytime by visiting your dashboard.

No new mentee will be assigned to you during your time off. Your mentees will be informed of your absence when they want to contact you, and they will be redirected to a different mentor. However, some newcomers who already started a conversation with you, or the ones that contact you by directly visiting your user talk page, may still leave you a message.

If you plan to be off for more than a few weeks, please consider to leave mentorship instead (and return!).

; How are newcomers filtered at my mentor dashboard? :By default, the Dashboard filter only displays users who've made at least one edit. Choosing a time period to show only those who've edited in the last day shortens it further. ; Can I choose who I want to be a mentor to? :In general, no. However, you can claim a mentee using [[Special:ClaimMentee]] (see [[:mw:Help:Growth/Tools/How to claim a mentee|How To Claim A Mentee]]). This tool can be used if you find a mentee who works on similar topics to you, or if you have an ongoing conversation with them.

If you only want to act as mentor to a few specific users (e.g. course participants/editathon attendees), you can sign up as a mentor following the above process, then visit [[Special:MentorDashboard]] and set the "Number of mentees assigned to me" dropdown to "None". You can still use [[Special:ClaimMentee]] but no new accounts will automatically be assigned to you.

; How can I leave mentorship? :If you want to leave mentorship for good and stop receiving new messages from your mentees, please visit [[Special:QuitMentorship]]. If you plan to return after a short absence, consider [[#away|pausing mentorship instead]].

Administrators may manage the list of mentors and their settings at [[Special:ManageMentors]].

;Where can I leave feedback? Where can I know more about mentorship and Growth features? : You may leave comments about the Mentor list at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features/Mentor list]], or give broader feedback and suggestions at [[Wikipedia talk:Growth Team features]]. You can also visit [[mw:Growth/FAQ|Growth's FAQ]]. {{Special:ManageMentors}}

comment: Reverted 1 edit by [[Special:Contributions/AAkurya|AAkurya]] ([[User talk:AAkurya|talk]]) to last revision by Oshwah
